article_title: Atom Computing Demonstrates Sustained Toric-Code Error Correction on a Neutral-Atom Quantum Processor

content_preview.title: Atom Computing Demonstrates Sustained Toric-Code Error Correction on a Neutral-Atom Quantum Processor
content_preview.summary: The company reports the first full toric-code error-correction demonstration on neutral atoms, sustaining logical memory across up to 90 cycles by reloading lost qubits from a reservoir.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Atom Computing has reported what it calls the industry's first full demonstration of quantum error correction using a toric code, run on its neutral-atom quantum computing system. According to the company's [June 3 announcement via PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/atom-computing-reveals-quantum-error-correction-with-toric-code-302789413.html), the result places Atom Computing among only two companies that have demonstrated many rounds of sustained quantum error ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Technically precise News piece (808 words) on Atom Computing's toric-code error-correction demonstration, careful to separate the peer-review-pending preprint result from the commercial announcements.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (arXiv abstract) confirms verbatim the syndrome-extraction/reservoir-reload quote, the 'logical error rate after up to 90 cycles' quote, and the 'two distances … up to 8 rounds … lower absolute logical error rate for the larger distance code' quote. Two body-only specifics — the 'currently configured to support 128 qubits' register-zone quote and the PyMatching decoder — are not on the abstract snapshot but were verified directly against the arXiv full-text HTML (arxiv.org/html/2606.04079v1), which carries both verbatim; the article cites the canonical arXiv paper URL per Rule 9. source-1 (PR Newswire) confirms the 'industry's first full demonstration … toric code' framing, the 'only two companies … many rounds of sustained quantum error correction … the first' wording, and all three expert quotes (Ben Bloom; Scott Aaronson, UT Austin; Matthias Troyer, Microsoft) plus the QuNorth/EIFO/Novo Nordisk/Magne/Microsoft, DARPA stage B, and $100M DOC LOI details. source-2 (Quantum Computing Report) confirms the Google superconducting comparison.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote is verbatim and correctly attributed to its speaker and outlet; every specific (90 cycles, 8 rounds, 128 qubits, PyMatching, $100M) traces to a source. The internal cross-reference to the March neutral-atom error-correction article was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately flags the preprint status and the inter-outlet disagreement on code parameters.
editor_notes.originality: New topic (Atom Computing's specific toric-code demonstration), cross-referencing but distinct from the March neutral-atom deployment roundup.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified quantum-computing submission with primary-source (arXiv) citation. APPROVE.
